attractions.name: Anyetsang's Little Tibet Restaurant
attractions.description: Anyetsang's Little Tibet has been serving authentic Tibetan, Thai, and Indian cuisine in Bloomington since 1998. Located in a charming converted bungalow on Fourth Street's "restaurant row," the restaurant offers a peaceful atmosphere with an outdoor patio featuring a fountain and prayer flags. Guests can enjoy a unique blend of flavors and cultures in the heart of downtown.
attractions.region: Downtown Bloomington / Fourth Street Restaurant Row
attractions.hours: Lunch: 11:00 AM - 3:00 PM daily; Dinner: 5:00 PM - 9:00 PM daily
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant)
highlights: Serving authentic Tibetan, Thai, and Indian cuisine since 1998.
Signature dishes include Mo Mo (handmade Tibetan dumplings) and Kham Amdo Thugpa (hearty stew).
Peaceful outdoor patio with a trickling fountain and traditional Tibetan prayer flags.
Customizable spice levels ranging from 1 to 5 for most entrees.
Complimentary lentil soup or salad included with lunch and dinner specialties.
Family-owned and operated in a cozy, converted bungalow setting.
attractions.tips: Be cautious with the spice levels as even level 1 can be quite spicy. The Momo dumplings are a local favorite and are made fresh daily. Outdoor seating in the garden is highly recommended during pleasant weather for a more serene dining experience. Reservations are advisable for larger groups.
attractions.nearby: Indiana University Bloomington, Nick's English Hut, The Upstairs Pub, Kilroy's on Kirkwood, Tibetan Mongolian Buddhist Cultural Center

attractions.name: Buskirk-Chumley Theater Official Website
attractions.description: The Buskirk-Chumley Theater, originally built in 1922 as a silent movie house called the Indiana Theatre, is a beautifully restored 630-seat historic venue in downtown Bloomington. Today, it serves as a vibrant community hub hosting over 200 events annually, including concerts, film screenings, theater productions, and local festivals Official About Page, Visit Bloomington

attractions.hours: Box Office: Tuesday - Friday: 11 am - 5 pm; Saturday: 12 pm - 5 pm; Sunday - Monday: Closed (except for performance days, opens 1 hour before showtime) Official Website
attractions.days_open: Tuesday through Saturday (Box Office); Sunday and Monday only for ticketed events Box Office Info
attractions.admission: Prices vary by event, typically ranging from $15 to $150+, with some community events starting around $38-$44 SeatGeek, Vivid Seats
highlights: Listed on the National Register of Historic Places Visit Bloomington
Originally built in 1922 as a Spanish Mission-style silent-movie house History Page
Primary venue for the Lotus World Music & Arts Festival and PRIDE Film Festival History Page
Managed by the non-profit BCT Management, Inc. since 2001 History Page

attractions.tips: Parking is metered 8am–9pm Monday–Saturday but free on Sundays; arrive at least 30 minutes early for will-call; there is no elevator in the historic building, so balcony access requires climbing steps Prepare For Your Visit, FAQ

attractions.name: Crumble Coffee & Bakery
attractions.description: Crumble Coffee & Bakery is a locally owned coffee shop and bakery in Bloomington, Indiana, known for its inviting atmosphere and high-quality offerings. They serve locally roasted coffee from Quarrymen Coffee Roasters, organic Hugo Tea, and a variety of handmade pastries, including their signature fruit-infused Crumble Bars and savory galettes. Official Website, Visit Bloomington

attractions.name: Function Brewing (formerly Metal Works Brewing Company)
attractions.description: Function Brewing was a popular downtown brewpub known for its experimental approach, having crafted over 220 unique beers since its opening in 2014. The establishment featured an airy, industrial atmosphere and a menu of gourmet, hand-crafted sandwiches and from-scratch soups that complemented its rotating tap list of mathematically-named beers. (B Square Bulletin, Visit Indiana)

Mathematically-themed beer names like "Theorem" (B Square Bulletin)

attractions.name: Grazie! Italiano
attractions.description: A long-standing Bloomington tradition, Grazie! Italian Eatery was a casual restaurant located in the heart of the downtown Square. Known for its authentic Italian cuisine and fresh-baked focaccia, the establishment featured an extensive wine selection with over 250 choices and used locally-sourced ingredients for its handmade dishes. It offered both indoor and outdoor seating, making it a favorite destination for locals and visitors alike before its transition to new ownership in 2021.
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 11:30 AM - 10:00 PM
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Fresh-baked focaccia served with extra-virgin olive oil with every meal.
Extensive wine cellar featuring a selection of over 250 domestic and imported wines.
Authentic Italian recipes prepared using locally-sourced ingredients and traditional methods.
Prime location on the historic Courthouse Square with popular outdoor patio seating.
Dedicated lounge area offering a wide range of domestic, imported, and local craft beers.
A diverse menu including seafood, steaks, and a wide variety of vegan and vegetarian options.
attractions.tips: Grazie! was a popular choice for date nights and family gatherings; it was often recommended for students to take visiting parents. For a true local experience, try their signature hand-rolled meatballs or any of their house-made pasta dishes. While the restaurant has since been replaced by Che Bello, it remains a fondly remembered part of Bloomington's culinary history.

attractions.description: Kirkwood Avenue is the most iconic street in Bloomington, serving as the vibrant heart of the city and the main connector between downtown and the Indiana University campus. The avenue is famous for its eclectic atmosphere, historic architecture, and a dense concentration of legendary bars, diverse restaurants, and unique local shops (Visit Bloomington, Airial Travel).

attractions.name: Lotus World Music & Arts Festival
attractions.description: The Lotus World Music & Arts Festival is a premier four-day event in downtown Bloomington that brings together artists from over 20 countries to celebrate global diversity through music and art. Established in 1994, it is one of the oldest and most respected world music festivals in the United States, offering a mix of ticketed showcases and free community activities. Lotus Education & Arts Foundation

highlights: International musical lineup featuring performers from over 20 countries.

attractions.tips: Purchase tickets early for 'Advance' pricing; wristbands are required for all ticketed venues. Parking is available in public garages (4th St, Walnut St, Morton St) for approximately $0.50 per hour. Use the mobile-friendly web schedule for real-time updates on artist performances. Lotus FAQ

attractions.name: Monroe County Courthouse
attractions.description: The Monroe County Courthouse is a historic Beaux-Arts building completed in 1908, known for its grand Indiana limestone exterior and distinctive copper-covered dome. Located in the heart of Bloomington's Courthouse Square Historic District, it serves as the seat of local government and is a recognized National Historic Place. The building's interior features elaborate murals, stained glass, and a restored rotunda that showcases the region's stone industry heritage. (Wikipedia), CFC Properties)
attractions.region: Courthouse Square Historic District, Downtown Bloomington

attractions.admission: Free (Yelp, Visit Bloomington)
highlights: Beaux-Arts classical architecture designed by Marshall and Guy Mahurin (CFC Properties)
Large copper-covered dome featuring a restored rotunda and murals by Gustav A. Brand (Wikipedia))
A historic copper weather vane shaped like a fish, saved from the original 1826 courthouse (Wikipedia))
Listing on the National Register of Historic Places since 1976 (Wikipedia))
Surrounding grounds featuring war memorials, including a Civil War monument and the "Freedom Flame" (Yelp, City of Bloomington)
Intricate stained glass windows in the Arts and Crafts style (City of Bloomington)
attractions.tips: Visitors can enjoy the murals and stained glass inside during business hours. Metered street parking is available on all four sides of the courthouse square. Be sure to look for the historic fish weather vane atop the dome. (Yelp, City of Bloomington)

attractions.name: Osteria Rago
attractions.description: Osteria Rago is an authentic Italian restaurant and deli nestled in a converted 1880s carriage house in downtown Bloomington, Indiana. The establishment focuses on providing a rustic and intimate dining experience with a menu that features house-made pasta, wood-fired pizzas, and seasonal Italian dishes. Located in a quiet alley just west of the Indiana University Sample Gates, it is often described as a hidden gem for its authentic atmosphere and fresh, organic ingredients.

Wood-fired Neapolitan-style pizzas and traditional Italian entrees.

attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended for dining in due to the small, cozy space. Visit during Happy Hour (Tuesday-Friday, 1 PM - 4 PM) for special deals. For parking, check the nearby Poplars Garage, which is often free after 5 PM on weekdays and all day on weekends. Be sure to check their rotating specials for seasonal dishes.

attractions.name: Rainbow Bakery
attractions.description: Rainbow Bakery is Bloomington's first all-vegan bakery, offering a vibrant and retro-themed space for a wide variety of plant-based treats. Specializing in cupcakes, donuts, cookies, and custom cakes, the bakery also provides gluten-free options and locally sourced drip coffee in a welcoming atmosphere.

Located in the historic I. Fell Building with colorful, retro-inspired decor.
Offers a diverse daily selection of donuts, cupcakes, cookies, brownies, and scones.
Dedicated separate display case and workstation for gluten-free baked goods.
Serves a variety of espresso, drip coffee, and cold brews with non-dairy milk options.
Available for custom cake orders, including unique photo designs and wedding cakes.

attractions.name: Sample Gates
attractions.description: The Sample Gates serve as the iconic entrance to Indiana University's Bloomington campus and the historic Old Crescent area. Funded by Edson Sample in 1987 and dedicated to his parents, the gates are constructed of Indiana limestone and represent the symbolic gateway between the university and the Bloomington community.
attractions.address: E Kirkwood Ave & S Indiana Ave, Bloomington, IN 47405
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Iconic entrance to the Indiana University Bloomington campus
Constructed from locally sourced Indiana limestone
Popular spot for graduation and commemorative photos
Marks the transition between the city of Bloomington and the university campus
Surroundings are typically adorned with seasonal flowers like tulips in the spring
Serves as a starting point for various campus traditions and public gatherings
attractions.tips: The gates are a prime photography spot, so bring a camera or a fully charged phone. Visit during the spring to see the blooming tulips or in the fall for the colorful foliage. Parking is available on nearby city streets or at the Indiana Memorial Union parking lot. It's a great starting point for a walking tour of the historic Old Crescent area.
attractions.nearby: Kirkwood Avenue (Downtown), Dunn Meadow, Dunn's Woods, Indiana Memorial Union (IMU), Eskenazi Museum of Art, Showalter Fountain, Rose Well House.

attractions.name: Waldron, Hill and Buskirk Park
attractions.description: Waldron, Hill and Buskirk Park, locally known as Third Street Park, is a vibrant 3.5-acre urban green space located in downtown Bloomington. The park serves as a community hub, featuring a modern playground, a historic ornamental fountain, and a performance stage that hosts various outdoor concerts and movies. It offers a serene environment for recreation and family gatherings within walking distance of downtown's main attractions (City of Bloomington).
attractions.hours: 5:00 AM - 11:00 PM (City of Bloomington)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Bloomington)
highlights: Modern playground with poured-in-place rubber surfacing (City of Bloomington)
Performance stage and bandstand with an arched canopy (OuterSpatial)
Historic ornamental fountain renovated from Rose Hill Cemetery (City of Bloomington)
Allison-Jukebox Community Center offering indoor activities (Bloomingpedia)
Large open green space for play and relaxation (Visit Bloomington)
Free public Wi-Fi for visitors (City of Bloomington)
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to bring their own seating for stage performances and take advantage of the rare free street parking available in the vicinity. It is a dog-friendly park, but pets must remain leashed at all times (City of Bloomington, The Project School).
attractions.nearby: Monroe County Public Library, The Chocolate Moose, Boys and Girls Club of Bloomington, and the Bloomington Police Headquarters (Bloomington Bucket List, Reddit)

attractions.name: Beck Chapel
attractions.description: Dedicated in 1957, Beck Chapel is a non-denominational sanctuary constructed of Indiana limestone and native wood from southern Indiana forests (News at IU). It serves as a space for prayer, meditation, and ceremonies, featuring symbols from various world religions to promote harmony and unity (Indiana Memorial Union).
attractions.region: Indiana University Campus / Old Crescent (Grant Street Inn)
attractions.hours: Open daily for prayer and meditation unless reserved for private events (Indiana Memorial Union)
attractions.admission: Free (Indiana Memorial Union)
highlights: Constructed with Indiana limestone and wood from southern Indiana forests (Indiana Memorial Union)
Houses sacred symbols representing Christian, Jewish, Buddhist, and Muslim faiths (Indiana University Archives)
Contains marble pieces from the Sistine Chapel and St. Paul’s Cathedral (News at IU)
Features six stained-glass windows designed by IU professor Rudy Pozzatti (News at IU)
Adjacent to the historic Dunn Cemetery, established in 1855 (Indiana Memorial Union)
Topped with a copper spire honoring fallen World War I and World War II soldiers (News at IU)
attractions.tips: The chapel is a quiet retreat for everyone on campus and is a popular spot for weddings and graduation photos (Indiana University Visitor Information Center). Check for private event reservations if planning a specific visit time (Indiana Memorial Union).
attractions.nearby: Indiana Memorial Union, Dunn Cemetery, Dunn Woods, Showalter Fountain, Eskenazi Museum of Art, Lilly Library (Visit Bloomington, Grant Street Inn)

attractions.name: Jesse H. and Beulah Chanley Cox Arboretum
attractions.description: The Jesse H. and Beulah Chanley Cox Arboretum is an 11-acre oasis on the Indiana University Bloomington campus that serves as a campus green space and living laboratory for botanical enthusiasts. It features a vast collection of trees and plant life from North America and around the world, providing a serene environment for students and visitors to relax or study (Indiana University).
attractions.address: Fee Lane and East 10th St, Bloomington, IN 47405
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Visit Bloomington)
attractions.admission: Free (Indiana University)
highlights: Arthur R. Metz Bicentennial Grand Carillon (128-foot tall bell tower) (Indiana University)
Jesse H. and Beulah Chanley Cox Pavilion (stone structure for shade and study) (Indiana University)
Site of the original Memorial Stadium (10th Street Stadium) (Visit Bloomington)
Extensive collection of native and exotic trees and plants (Indiana University)
Numerous walking paths and scenic picnic spots (Visit Bloomington)
Featured in the 1979 film "Breaking Away" (Indiana University)
attractions.tips: The arboretum is a prime spot to enjoy a picnic or relax in a hammock on a warm day (Visit Bloomington). Visitors should check for carillon recitals at the Metz Grand Carillon for a unique musical experience (Chamber of Commerce).
attractions.nearby: Herman B Wells Library, Eskenazi Museum of Art, Kelley School of Business, School of Public Health (Visit Bloomington)

attractions.name: Kirkwood Observatory
attractions.description: Constructed in 1900 and dedicated in 1901, the Kirkwood Observatory is a historic astronomical facility located on the Indiana University Bloomington campus. It serves as a center for public outreach and education, housing a historic 12-inch refracting telescope and a dedicated solar telescope for viewing celestial bodies and the sun (Indiana University, Wikipedia).
attractions.region: Indiana University Campus / Dunn's Woods (Indiana University)
attractions.hours: Public Open Nights: Wednesdays from after Spring Break until late November (weather permitting). Start times vary by season: 9:00 PM – 10:30 PM (March/April), 10:00 PM – 11:30 PM (May–August), 9:00 PM – 10:30 PM (September–November). Solar Viewing: Select Saturdays (typically first of the month) 1:00 PM – 3:00 PM (Indiana University, Indiana Daily Student)
attractions.days_open: Wednesdays (Public Open Nights), select Saturdays (Solar Viewing) (Indiana University)
attractions.admission: Free (Indiana University)
highlights: Houses a historic 0.3-meter (12-inch) refracting telescope with optics ground by Simon Brashear (Indiana University)
Features a solar telescope for public viewing of sunspots, solar flares, and prominences (Indiana University)
Named after renowned astronomer Daniel Kirkwood, discoverer of the 'Kirkwood gaps' in the asteroid belt (Indiana University)
Located in the scenic Dunn’s Woods at the western edge of the Indiana University campus (Indiana University)
Maintains a historic Howard Clock in the lobby, originally used for timing binary star observations (Indiana University)
attractions.tips: Check the official website or @iuastro on social media two hours before visiting to confirm the observatory is open, as viewings are weather-dependent. The building is not handicapped-accessible and requires climbing stairs to reach the telescope (Indiana University).
attractions.nearby: Sample Gates, Dunn's Woods, Indiana Memorial Union, Eskenazi Museum of Art, Kirkwood Avenue (Visit Bloomington)

attractions.name: Merchants Bank Field at Memorial Stadium
attractions.description: Memorial Stadium is a 53,524-seat American football stadium in Bloomington, Indiana, and the home of the Indiana University Hoosiers. Opened in 1960, it serves as the centerpiece of the IU Athletics complex and features modern facilities such as the Terry Tallen Indiana Football Complex and George Taliaferro Plaza. Wikipedia), Indiana University Athletics
attractions.region: Indiana University Campus (Northeast corner of 17th St and N Dunn St)
attractions.hours: Business Hours: Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Gameday: Gates open 90 minutes before kickoff. Tripadvisor, IU Today
attractions.admission: Season tickets range from $458 to $1,082; single-game tickets start at approximately $58. Gameday festival area (B-Town Boulevard) is free to the public. SeatGeek, Reddit
highlights: Home of the Indiana University Hoosiers football team Wikipedia)
Features "The Rock," a two-ton limestone boulder from a local quarry Wikipedia)
George Taliaferro Plaza honors the first African American drafted by an NFL team Indiana University Athletics
B-Town Boulevard gameday festival with live music and food trucks IU Today
Terry Tallen Indiana Football Complex featuring advanced training facilities Indiana University Athletics
Excellence Academy providing top-tier student-athlete support Indiana University Athletics
attractions.tips: Arrive early to witness "The Walk" tradition 2 hours and 25 minutes before kickoff. Follow the clear bag policy to ensure smooth entry, and check out the B-Town Boulevard 2.5 hours before the game for pre-game entertainment. IU Today
attractions.nearby: Simon Skjodt Assembly Hall, Indiana University Arboretum, Indiana Memorial Union, Sample Gates, Eskenazi Museum of Art. Visit Bloomington

attractions.name: Soma Coffeehouse and Juice Bar
attractions.description: Established in 1997, Soma Coffeehouse and Juice Bar is a quirky local favorite in Bloomington known for its "underground grunge classic" vibe and commitment to high-quality coffee and juices. It serves a diverse community of students, artists, and locals with an extensive menu of signature espressos, smoothies, and fresh-baked pastries.
attractions.address: 322 E Kirkwood Ave (Kirkwood), 1400 E 3rd St (East), 581 E Hillside Dr Suite 104 (Hillside)
attractions.hours: Kirkwood: Mon-Wed 6a-7p, Thu-Fri 6a-9p, Sat 7a-9p, Sun 7a-7p; East: Weekdays 6a-9p, Weekends 7a-9p; Hillside: Weekdays 6a-6p, Weekends 7a-6p
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Vibrant underground and kitschy atmosphere at the flagship Kirkwood location
Wide selection of signature espressos like the 'Oahu' and house-blended organic juices
Fresh-baked pastries including croissants, muffins, and bagels
Commitment to community activism and partnerships with local organizations
Convenient locations near the Indiana University campus and B-Line Trail
Backroom entrance to Moonstones and TD’s CDs & LPs at the Kirkwood shop
attractions.tips: The Kirkwood location is perfect for a cozy study session but can get busy; visit the Third Street location for more sunlight. If you're at Kirkwood, check out the record shop and crystal store attached to the back. Parking can be difficult on Sunday mornings due to nearby churches, so consider walking or biking.
attractions.nearby: Indiana University campus, B-Line Trail, Switchyard Park, Moonstones, TD’s CDs & LPs, The Laughing Planet, Village Deli, Indiana Shop

attractions.name: FARMbloomington
attractions.description: FARMbloomington is a farm-to-table restaurant founded in 2008 by Chef Daniel Orr in the historic Oddfellows building. It offers seasonally inspired American classics using local ingredients from Southern Indiana, featuring a Market Café, a main dining room, and the subterranean Root Cellar Lounge. Official Website
attractions.hours: Tuesday-Thursday: 10:30 AM - 9:00 PM; Friday-Saturday: 9:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Sunday: 9:00 AM - 9:00 PM (Note: Limited lunch 3-5 PM; Root Cellar Bar Wed-Sat 9 PM-Late). Official Location Page
attractions.admission: Free to visit; Meal prices vary (e.g., Thanksgiving feast ~$62/person, Graduation brunch/dinner ~$35-$85/person). Official Menus
highlights: Seasonally inspired dinner menus and daily specials by Chef Daniel Orr. Source
The Root Cellar, a subterranean speakeasy and vintage entertainment "cave." Source
Commitment to sustainability, using local honey, sorghum, and foraged herbs. Source
Eclectic dining space within the historic Oddfellows building. Source
Full bar featuring fresh local ingredients and craft cocktails. Source
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended, especially during peak seasons and IU graduation weekends. The Root Cellar is a 'best kept secret' for late-night entertainment. Official Website
attractions.nearby: Kirkwood Avenue, Indiana University Sample Gates, Nick's English Hut, Uptown Cafe, Village Deli. Visit Bloomington

attractions.name: The Village Deli
attractions.description: The Village Deli is a beloved local breakfast and lunch spot in Bloomington, known for its classic diner-style atmosphere and hearty portions since the 1980s. It is famous for its 'ginormous' pancakes and 'Eat and Get Out' motto, serving Indiana University students and locals alike in the heart of the Kirkwood Avenue district.
attractions.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Thursday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Friday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Saturday: 8:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Sunday: 8:00 AM – 3:00 PM
attractions.days_open: Monday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Closed Tuesdays)
attractions.admission: Free (Menu prices typically range from $1.35 for sides up to ~$15 for platters)
highlights: Famous 'Ginormous' oversized buttermilk pancakes
Paxton's Potatoes: home-fried potatoes with sausage gravy, cheddar, and chives
Breakfast served all day long
Homemade granola and farm-fresh egg dishes
Hall of Fame sandwiches, beefy burgers, and homemade soups
Located steps from the Indiana University Bloomington campus
attractions.tips: Expect a wait on weekend mornings as it is a popular student and tourist destination. If you're sensitive to crowds, try arriving before 9:00 AM. Don't miss the Cinnamon Roll Pancakes or Paxton's Potatoes, which are local favorites. The restaurant is known for its quick service and 'Eat and Get Out' philosophy during busy times.
attractions.nearby: Indiana University Bloomington, Nick's English Hut, Kilroy's on Kirkwood, The Upstairs Pub, Sample Gates, B-Line Trail

attractions.name: Hardin Ridge Recreation Area (USDA Forest Service)
attractions.description: Hardin Ridge Recreation Area is a 1,200-acre complex located on the shores of Lake Monroe within the Hoosier National Forest. It offers a diverse range of outdoor activities including camping across six loops, swimming at a 300-foot sandy beach, and boating via a three-lane ramp. The area is heavily forested with hardwoods, providing scenic views and interpretive trails for nature enthusiasts. (Recreation.gov)
attractions.address: 6464 Hardin Ridge Road, Heltonville, IN 47436 (USDA Forest Service)
attractions.region: Hoosier National Forest / Monroe County (USDA Forest Service)
attractions.hours: Trails: Open 24/7 year-round. Beach: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM (Seasonally). Campground: Check-in 2:00 PM, Check-out 12:00 PM. Quiet Hours: 10:00 PM – 6:00 AM. (USDA Forest Service, Recreation.gov)
attractions.days_open: Trails: Year-round; Campground/Beach/Gatehouse: Daily from mid-April through October (USDA Forest Service)
attractions.admission: Day Use: $8 per vehicle; Annual Day Use Pass: $50; Camping: $21–$31 per night; Cabin Rentals: $55 per night (USDA Forest Service)
highlights: 300-foot swimming beach and bathhouse on Lake Monroe (Recreation.gov)
Two-mile Hardin Ridge Trail for hiking and mountain biking (USDA Forest Service)
Three-lane boat launch ramp with a courtesy dock (Recreation.gov)
Scenic overlook with views of Indiana's largest reservoir (Recreation.gov)
1.2-mile Ted T. Turtle interpretive trail through hardwood forest (Recreation.gov)
Over 200 campsites and two primitive lakefront rental cabins (Visit Bloomington)
attractions.tips: Pets are strictly prohibited on the swimming beach and must be leashed elsewhere. Visitors are encouraged to bring locally sourced firewood to protect against invasive species. Raccoons are active in the area, so ensure all food and trash are secured. (USDA Forest Service, Recreation.gov)
attractions.nearby: Brooks Cabin, Charles C. Deam Wilderness, Hickory Ridge Lookout Tower, Monroe Lake Marinas (Recreation.gov)

attractions.name: Indiana University Bloomington
attractions.description: Indiana University Bloomington is a prestigious public research university and the flagship campus of the Indiana University system. Established in 1820, it is renowned for its stunning limestone architecture, vibrant student life, and world-class programs in music, business, and the arts.
attractions.hours: Monday–Friday: 9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.; Saturday: 10:00 a.m. – 2:00 p.m.; Sunday: 12:00 p.m. – 3:00 p.m. (Visitor Information Center hours)
attractions.admission: Free (Campus visit); $65 (Application fee)
highlights: Sample Gates: The iconic limestone entryway to the university's historic Old Crescent.
Indiana Memorial Union: One of the world's largest student unions, featuring a hotel, art collection, and various amenities.
Eskenazi Museum of Art: A world-class museum designed by I.M. Pei, housing over 40,000 objects.
Herman B Wells Library: A massive research library with over 4.6 million volumes.
Cox Arboretum: A 11-acre green space in the heart of campus perfect for relaxation and study.
Rose Well House: A historic Gothic Revival structure and a popular spot for student traditions.
attractions.tips: Start your visit at the IU Visitor Information Center in the Indiana Memorial Union for maps and local advice. Wear comfortable walking shoes to explore the nearly 2,000-acre campus. Don't miss the chance to shake hands with the statue of Herman B Wells for good luck.
attractions.nearby: Kirkwood Avenue, Monroe Lake, Brown County State Park, Oliver Winery, McCormick's Creek State Park

attractions.name: Lake Lemon
attractions.description: Lake Lemon is a 1,650-acre reservoir located approximately 10 miles northeast of Bloomington, Indiana, spanning Monroe and Brown counties (Wikipedia). Built in 1953 as a primary water source, it now serves as a popular recreational destination for boating, fishing, and wildlife viewing (Visit Bloomington). The lake features 24 miles of shoreline and is recognized as one of the state's most productive bass fisheries (Lake Lemon Conservancy District).
attractions.hours: 8:00 AM - 8:00 PM daily (Memorial Day to Labor Day); limited amenities during off-season (Lake Lemon Conservancy District, Live Like Karlie)
attractions.days_open: Open daily; peak season with full amenities from Memorial Day through Labor Day (Live Like Karlie, Lake Lemon Conservancy District)
attractions.admission: $12.00 per motorized vehicle (Daily), $85.00 per motorized vehicle (Seasonal Pass) (Lake Lemon Conservancy District)
highlights: Riddle Point Park, featuring a public beach, bathhouse, playground, and shelter house rentals (Lake Lemon Conservancy District)
Top-rated bass fishery and home to nearly two dozen fish species (Visit Bloomington)
Little Africa Wildlife Viewing Area, a 24-acre peninsula ideal for birding and nature study (Wikipedia)
Home to Indiana University's Women's Rowing Team and local sailing clubs (Visit Bloomington)
Tulip Trace Nature Trail, offering a 0.5-mile loop with scenic lake overlooks (Visit Bloomington)
Shuffle Creek Trestle, a notable local landmark on the south side of the lake (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Bring your own watercraft but be sure to purchase a boating permit; stop by Butler Winery's vineyard or The Porthole Inn for local dining just five minutes away (Visit Bloomington). Arrive during peak season (Memorial Day to Labor Day) to access the swimming beach and bathhouse facilities (Lake Lemon Conservancy District).
attractions.nearby: Butler Winery (Vineyard), The Porthole Inn, Yellowwood State Forest, Indiana University, Morgan-Monroe State Forest (Visit Bloomington, Lake Homes Realty)

attractions.name: Leonard Springs Nature Park
attractions.description: Leonard Springs Nature Park is a 95.5-acre natural area located southwest of Bloomington, featuring a diverse landscape of wetlands, caves, and forestry. The park offers a rugged 1.1-mile loop trail that provides visitors with views of Leonard and Shirley Springs, as well as unique karst topography. It is designed primarily for nature observation and environmental education, highlighting the area's rich geological and ecological history. (City of Bloomington)
attractions.region: Southwest Bloomington / Monroe County (City of Bloomington)
attractions.hours: Monday: 5 a.m. - 11 p.m., Tuesday: 5 a.m. - 11 p.m., Wednesday: 5 a.m. - 11 p.m., Thursday: 5 a.m. - 11 p.m., Friday: 5 a.m. - 11 p.m., Saturday: 5 a.m. - 11 p.m., Sunday: 5 a.m. - 11 p.m. (City of Bloomington)
attractions.admission: Free (City of Bloomington)
highlights: Leonard and Shirley Springs issuing from natural alcoves
Two caves viewable from the main hiking trail
1.1-mile rugged loop trail with stairs and steep slopes
Diverse habitats including wetlands, forests, and springs
Historical landmarks such as an abandoned concrete water tower
Scenic seasonal features like frozen waterfalls in winter
attractions.tips: The terrain is very rugged and not handicap accessible; sturdy hiking boots are highly recommended. No drinkable water is available on-site, so visitors should bring their own. Restrooms are vault-type and only open seasonally. Visitors must stay on the designated wood-chipped trails to avoid disturbing the sensitive local flora and fauna. (City of Bloomington, Airial Travel)
attractions.nearby: Wapehani Mountain Bike Park, WonderLab Science Museum, Monroe County History Center, Tibetan Mongolian Buddhist Cultural Center, Indiana University Bloomington (Visit Bloomington)

attractions.name: Monroe Lake (Monroe Reservoir)
attractions.description: Monroe Lake is Indiana's largest land-bound body of water, spanning approximately 10,750 surface acres. Managed as a cooperative effort between the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers and the Indiana DNR, it serves as a vital resource for flood control, water supply, and extensive outdoor recreation. The property features nine state recreation areas providing access to boating, fishing, hiking, and camping amidst the surrounding Hoosier National Forest. Visit Bloomington, Indiana DNR Official Site
attractions.hours: Property is open daily year-round. Specific facility hours (e.g., Paynetown Activity Center) vary seasonally: Memorial Day to mid-August, Fri-Sun 1-5 PM; mid-August to October, Sat-Sun 1-5 PM; Closed November to late May. Indiana DNR
attractions.admission: $7 In-State Daily Entrance, $15 Out-of-State Daily Entrance (per noncommercial vehicle); $2 per person for pedestrians, cyclists, or those on horseback. Annual passes are available for $50 (Resident) or $120 (Non-resident). Indiana DNR
highlights: Indiana's largest lake with over 10,750 acres of water surface and 103 miles of shoreline. Friends of Lake Monroe
Home to nine diverse State Recreation Areas (SRAs) including the highly developed Fairfax and Paynetown sites. Indiana DNR
Excellent fishing opportunities for bluegill, largemouth bass, and crappie across both open and no-wake areas. Visit Bloomington
Extensive trail system for hiking and wildlife viewing, including the Bluebird and Whitetail trails. Visit Bloomington
Significant birdwatching destination, recognized as a stop on the Indiana Birding Trail with frequent eagle sightings. Indiana DNR
Access to the Charles C. Deam Wilderness, offering nearly 13,000 acres of primitive and wild forest land. Visit Bloomington
attractions.tips: Be aware that swimming beaches are exclusively located at the Fairfax and Paynetown State Recreation Areas. Visitors should check water levels before arrival as heavy rains can lead to facility closures, and be mindful of 'Black Vultures' in parking lots which have been known to damage vehicle trim. An Indiana State Fishing License is required for all anglers ages 18 and older. Indiana DNR, Visit Bloomington
attractions.nearby: Fourwinds Lakeside Inn & Marina, Hoosier National Forest, Charles C. Deam Wilderness, Cedar Bluffs Nature Preserve, and Brown County State Park. Visit Bloomington, Fourwinds Lakeside Inn

attractions.name: Oliver Winery & Vineyards
attractions.description: Oliver Winery is Indiana's oldest and largest winery, consistently ranked among the top 25 vineyards to visit in the United States. Founded in 1972, it offers a refreshingly real approach to winemaking with a focus on approachable, fruit-forward wines. Visitors can enjoy beautiful gardens, world-class tasting experiences, and scenic picnic grounds.
attractions.hours: Retail Hours: Sunday–Thursday (12:00 PM – 6:00 PM), Friday–Saturday (11:00 AM – 8:00 PM); Tasting Hours: Sunday–Thursday (12:00 PM – 5:30 PM), Friday–Saturday (11:00 AM – 7:30 PM) (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Guided Wine Tastings: $5.00 (Tripadvisor), Patio Wine Flights: $15.00 (Yelp), Reserved Wine Tastings: $21.60 per person (Official Website), Picnic Experience: $29.16 per person (CellarPass), Tour and Tasting Experience: $37.80 per person (CellarPass)
highlights: Ranked as a top 25 winery in the U.S. by Travel + Leisure (Visit Bloomington)
Extensive wine portfolio including the popular Soft Red and Real Fruit Moscato collections (Official Website)
Beautiful 15-acre grounds featuring gardens, a pond, and a timber-frame tasting room (GoPetFriendly)
Exclusive production and cellar tours showing the 'vine to wine' process (Official Website)
Curated picnic packages featuring artisan charcuterie and reserved patio seating (Official Website)
Home to the 54-acre Creekbend Vineyard in the Indiana Uplands AVA (CU4Wine)
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended for tours and picnics as they sell out quickly. Leashed pets are welcome on the hillside area but not inside the tasting room. Guests are allowed to bring their own picnics to the hillside area, but only Oliver wines can be consumed on-site. (Official Website, Indy with Kids)
attractions.nearby: Indiana University Bloomington, WonderLab Science Museum, Hoosier National Forest, Monroe Lake, McCormick's Creek State Park (Visit Bloomington)

attractions.name: Bartholomew County Courthouse
attractions.description: The Bartholomew County Courthouse is a historic Second Empire-style building designed by noted architect Isaac Hodgson and completed in 1874. It is a centerpiece of Columbus, Indiana's architectural landscape, featuring a grand 154-foot clock tower and red-brick masonry trimmed with limestone. Wikipedia, Indiana Historical Bureau
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM, Tuesday: 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM, Wednesday: 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM, Thursday: 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM, Friday: 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM, Saturday: Closed, Sunday: Closed Bartholomew County Records
attractions.admission: Free (Public government building) Bartholomew County Records
highlights: Designed by architect Isaac Hodgson in the Second Empire style with red brick and limestone trim. Wikipedia
Features a prominent six-level, 154-foot clock tower with a bell installed in 1875. Wikipedia
Listed on the National Register of Historic Places as a contributing property to the Columbus Historic District. NRHP
Adjacent to the Bartholomew County Veterans Memorial, designed by Charles Rose. Tripadvisor
Known as the 'Grand Dam' of 'Athens on the Prairie,' representing the community's historic prosperity. Indiana Landmarks
attractions.tips: As an active government building, expect security screenings upon entry; it is closed on weekends for interior viewing, though the exterior and grounds are always accessible for photography. Roadtirement
attractions.nearby: Bartholomew County Veterans Memorial, Zaharakos Ice Cream Parlor, The Commons, Bartholomew County Public Library, Mill Race Park Visit Columbus Indiana

attractions.name: Bloomington Community Farmers' Market (City of Bloomington)
attractions.description: The Bloomington Community Farmers' Market is a vibrant weekly gathering where shoppers find a wide assortment of local goods from farm and field, including fresh produce, meats, cheeses, and artisan crafts. It serves as a meaningful way to connect with the community and support local farmers and small businesses while enjoying live music, busker performances, and prepared foods (City of Bloomington, Visit Bloomington).
attractions.address: 401 N. Morton St. (April-October); 1601 S. Rogers St. (November) (City of Bloomington)
attractions.hours: Saturday (April-Sept): 8:00 AM – 12:30 PM (Showers Common); Saturday (Oct): 9:00 AM – 12:30 PM (Showers Common); Saturday (Nov): 9:00 AM – 12:30 PM (Switchyard Park Pavilion); Tuesday (June-Sept): 4:00 PM – 7:00 PM (Hopewell Commons); Winter Market (Dec-March): Saturdays 9:00 AM – 12:30 PM (Switchyard Park Pavilion) (City of Bloomington, Visit Bloomington)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Bloomington)

Prepared food and beverage artisans offering a variety of drinks, pastries, and savory dishes (City of Bloomington)
Weekly live music performances and buskers, including balloon artists and jugglers (Visit Bloomington)
Special events like Youth Day, tomato tastings, and salsa contests (City of Bloomington)
Integration with nutrition assistance programs including SNAP, WIC, and Senior FMNP (City of Bloomington)
Convenient location right next to the B-Line Trail for easy walking and biking access (Visit Bloomington)
attractions.tips: Arrive early for the best selection as popular items like corn and pastries sell out fast. Bring cash and reusable bags, as not all vendors accept cards. Note that pets, smoking, and vaping are not permitted within the market boundaries (Visit Bloomington, Airial Travel).
attractions.nearby: B-Line Trail, WonderLab Science Museum, Bloomington Antique Mall, and various downtown shops and restaurants (Visit Bloomington)

attractions.name: Bloomington Playwrights Project (Constellation Playhouse)
attractions.description: The Bloomington Playwrights Project (BPP) is a professional theatre dedicated solely to the production and development of new plays and musicals. Founded in 1979, it merged with Cardinal Stage and Pigasus Institute in 2022 to form Constellation Stage & Screen, continuing its legacy in the renovated 86-seat Constellation Playhouse (Constellation Stage & Screen).
attractions.hours: Box Office (Phone): Monday–Friday, 12:00 PM – 5:00 PM; Box Office (In-Person at Waldron Arts Center): Wednesday–Friday, 12:00 PM – 5:00 PM; Performance times vary by show (Constellation Stage & Screen).
attractions.days_open: Performance days (typically Thursday through Sunday during show runs); Box Office is available Monday through Friday (Constellation Stage & Screen).
attractions.admission: Mainstage single tickets average approximately $35 (Constellation Stage & Screen). Pay What You Will Thursdays start at $10 (plus a $3 fee). All orders include a $2 convenience fee and a $1-$2 venue preservation fee (Constellation Stage & Screen).
highlights: Sole professional theatre in Indiana dedicated exclusively to new plays and musicals (Constellation Stage & Screen).
Has premiered over 550 original works since its founding in 1979 (Behance-Logo)).
Features the 86-seat Timothy J. Wiles Mainstage and the Lora Shiner Studio Stage (Wikipedia).
Hosts the McCarty New Works Initiative to support the creation of world-premiere productions (Constellation Stage & Screen).
Offers extensive theatre education programs for children and teens ages 5-18 (Bloomington Volunteer Network).
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 15 minutes before showtime as there is no late seating. Avoid parking in the Big Red or College Square lots to prevent towing; the Walnut Street Garage is the recommended parking option (Constellation Stage & Screen). Take advantage of Pay What You Will Thursdays for affordable ticket options (Constellation Stage & Screen).
attractions.nearby: Buskirk-Chumley Theater, Waldron Arts Center, Runcible Spoon Restaurant, Bloomington Entertainment and Arts District (BEAD) (Constellation Stage & Screen).

attractions.name: Brown County State Park
attractions.description: Nicknamed the “Little Smokies” for its resemblance to the Great Smoky Mountains, Brown County State Park spans nearly 16,000 acres of rugged hills and deep ravines. As Indiana's largest state park, it is a premier destination for fall foliage, offering scenic vistas, extensive hiking and mountain biking trails, and the rustic Abe Martin Lodge.
attractions.address: 1801 State Road 46 East (North Gate), 1405 State Road 46 West (West Gate)
attractions.hours: Daily: 7:00 AM – 11:00 PM (Park Gates); Nature Center: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM daily

attractions.name: Dunn's Woods
attractions.description: Dunn's Woods is a serene, 10-acre woodland plot located in the heart of Indiana University Bloomington's "Old Crescent." Known for its centuries-old trees, charming brick sidewalks, and rich history as part of the original Dunn family farm, it serves as a tranquil natural escape on campus. The area is a passion project of former IU President Herman B Wells and remains a central icon of the university's "woodland campus" identity.

attractions.admission: Free

Dunn Cemetery - A private family cemetery preserved within the woods for the original owners.
Sample Gates - The iconic limestone entrance to the IU campus bordering the woods.
Diverse Native Flora - Home to native wildflowers like Trout Lily, Trillium, and Spring Beauty.
attractions.tips: Visit in the spring to see native wildflowers in bloom or in the winter for a peaceful snow-covered landscape. The Herman B Wells statue is a must-see for a classic IU photo opportunity. Parking is available in nearby campus garages like the IMU or Henderson garages (fees apply).

attractions.name: Indiana University Auditorium
attractions.description: Opened in 1941, the Indiana University Auditorium is a premier 3,200-seat performing arts venue located on the IU Bloomington campus. It hosts a diverse range of programming including touring Broadway productions, world-class concerts, comedians, and notable public speakers. The venue serves as a cultural cornerstone for the university and the broader South Central Indiana community (Official Website, Wikipedia).

attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by event; single tickets typically start at $25 for adults and $15 for IU Bloomington students. Additional tiers include season subscriptions (up to 24% off), group rates for 10+ people (approx. $22 per ticket), and $10 Student Rush tickets available on show days (Official Website, IU Theatre).
highlights: Thomas Hart Benton Murals: Features the historic "Century of Progress" murals in the Hall of Murals (Official Website).
Roosevelt Organ: Houses a massive pipe organ originally from the Chicago Auditorium Theatre (Wikipedia).
3,200-Seat Capacity: One of the region's largest venues for major entertainment events (Visit Bloomington).

Broadway Touring Acts: A regular stop for major national Broadway tours (Official Website).
Limestone Architecture: Constructed from locally-quarried Indiana limestone in a classic collegiate style (Visit Bloomington).
attractions.tips: Arrive early to view the historic Benton Murals in the foyer. IU Bloomington students should bring their valid student ID to use discounted tickets and can charge purchases directly to their bursar account. Short-term 15-minute parking is available in front of the building for box office visits (Official Website).

attractions.description: Lower Cascades Park is Bloomington's first city park, originally dedicated in 1924 and now listed on the Indiana Register of Historic Sites and Structures. Located in the valley of Cascades Creek, the 93.9-acre park features scenic waterfalls, historic limestone shelters built by the WPA, and a massive inclusive playground. attractions.description: The Monroe County Courthouse is a magnificent Beaux Arts landmark in the heart of downtown Bloomington, completed in 1908 and constructed from local Indiana limestone. Its iconic copper-covered dome and intricate interior murals by artist Gustav A. Brand reflect the community's rich history in education and the stone industry. The building and its surrounding square serve as the center of the Courthouse Square Historic District, hosting numerous memorials and serving as a focal point for the city's social and governmental life.

attractions.description: The Musical Arts Center (MAC) at Indiana University is one of the premier performance venues in the United States, known for its striking Brutalist architecture and world-class acoustics. Completed in 1972, it serves as the primary home for the IU Jacobs School of Music’s opera and ballet productions. The facility features a massive stage comparable to the Metropolitan Opera in New York and seats over 1,400 patrons (Visit Bloomington, Jacobs School of Music).

It features a extensive campground, a sandy swimming beach, a full-service marina, and three scenic hiking trails. The site is also home to the Monroe Lake Interpretive Center, which offers year-round nature programs and educational activities for visitors.

Situated on 211 acres in the rolling hills of Brown County, the site offers a glimpse into the life and work of one of Indiana's most significant landscape painters. Visitors can explore the historic "House of the Singing Winds," tour the large studio filled with original artwork, and hike through a 92-acre nature preserve. Official Site

Downtown Art Galleries and Artisan Shops (Art Colony)
Hard Truth Distilling Co. and Big Woods Village
Historic Brown County Courthouse and Pioneer Village
attractions.tips: Downtown Nashville is highly walkable, so find a parking spot and explore on foot. Peak visitation occurs in October for fall foliage; plan for crowds. RVs and large trailers must use the West Gate for Brown County State Park due to height restrictions on the historic covered bridge (Facebook, Brown County State Park).
attractions.nearby: Monroe Lake, Hoosier National Forest, City of Bloomington, Bean Blossom Covered Bridge, Story, IN (Caffeinated Excursions)

attractions.name: Upland Brewing Company (uplandbeer.com)
attractions.description: Upland Brewing Company's headquarters in Bloomington is a throwback to pubs of old, serving as a gathering place for friends and neighbors since 1998. It offers a variety of craft beers including wood-aged sour ales, traditional ales, and lagers, paired with a menu featuring locally sourced ingredients and vegetarian options. (uplandbeer.com)
attractions.hours: Mon-Thu: 11AM - 10PM, Fri-Sat: 11AM - 11PM, Sun: 11AM - 10PM (Brunch: Sat-Sun: 11AM - 2PM) (uplandbeer.com)
attractions.admission: Free (visitbloomington.com)
highlights: Pioneer of Indiana's craft beer scene since 1998 (getbrewledger.com)
Renowned sour beer program with dedicated Wood Shop facility (sourbeerblog.com)
Dog-friendly and family-friendly patio open Spring through Fall (uplandbeer.com)
Menu featuring locally sourced ingredients and creative vegetarian options (uplandbeer.com)
Official brewer of Champagne Velvet, a classic Indiana pilsner (getbrewledger.com)
attractions.tips: The kitchen stays open until 10 PM every night. For limited sour beer releases, check their lottery system or join the Secret Barrel Society for guaranteed access. The patio is a popular spot during warmer months and welcomes both children and dogs. (tripadvisor.com, sourbeerblog.com)
attractions.nearby: Building and Trades Park, Indiana University Bloomington, WonderLab, Hoosier Escape House (tripadvisor.com)

attractions.name: Winslow Woods Park
attractions.description: Winslow Woods Park is a 39-acre nature park in Bloomington, Indiana, characterized by its mature woods and unique sinkhole features. The park provides a variety of amenities, including a 0.74-mile natural surface trail, a basketball court, and a playground with modern rubber safety surfacing. It is also a center for local agriculture, hosting the Willie Streeter Community Garden and sitting adjacent to the Bloomington Community Orchard. (Official Website, OuterSpatial)
attractions.hours: Sunday: 5 a.m. - 11 p.m., Monday: 5 a.m. - 11 p.m., Tuesday: 5 a.m. - 11 p.m., Wednesday: 5 a.m. - 11 p.m., Thursday: 5 a.m. - 11 p.m., Friday: 5 a.m. - 11 p.m., Saturday: 5 a.m. - 11 p.m. (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Shelter rentals and special programs like Hike & Seek require fees: Official Website)
highlights: 39-acre nature park featuring mature woods and several sinkholes (Official Website)
0.74-mile natural/dirt surface trail for hiking and wildlife watching (OuterSpatial)
Modern playground with poured-in-place rubber safety surfacing, updated in 2021 (Official Website)
Home to the Willie Streeter Community Garden, offering rental plots for residents (Official Website)
Full-size asphalt basketball court and large open play field (OuterSpatial)
Large reservable picnic shelter equipped with grills, electricity, and lights (Official Website)
attractions.tips: The park's natural surface trails can be uneven and are best explored with appropriate footwear. It is a popular spot for bird watching, and public transportation is available via Bloomington Transit #4. For those interested in gardening, community plots are typically available for rent from April through October. (Official Website, OuterSpatial)
attractions.nearby: Willie Streeter Community Garden (on-site), Bloomington Community Orchard (adjacent), Winslow Sports Complex, YMCA of Monroe County Southeast (Official Website)

attractions.name: WonderLab Museum of Science, Health & Technology
attractions.description: WonderLab Museum of Science, Health & Technology is an award-winning science museum that provides hands-on exhibits and programs to stimulate curiosity and exploration for people of all ages. Located in a 15,000-square-foot facility, it features two floors of interactive discoveries focused on science, health, and technology Wikipedia, WonderLab.
attractions.region: Bloomington Entertainment and Arts District (BEAD) Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed (Open on select holidays like MLK Day); Tuesday–Thursday: 9am – 6pm; Friday–Saturday: 9am – 5pm; Sunday: 1pm – 5pm WonderLab
attractions.admission: Members: Free, Non-members (age 1+): $14.50, Access Pass Program: $5, Infants (under age 1): Free WonderLab
highlights: Grapevine Climber: A giant two-story climbing structure where children can climb up giant leaves Wikipedia
Fitzgerald Hall of Natural Science: Showcases live insects, amphibians, and reptiles in their natural habitats Visit Bloomington
Bubble Flow: Interactive exhibits for exploring "soapy science" through bubbles Visit Bloomington
WonderGarden: An outdoor nature area with science exhibits and an amphitheater Wikipedia
Beehive: A 5-foot tall engineering exhibit where visitors can test their building skills WonderLab
Hidden Life of Deserts: An exhibit featuring desert creatures and their environments WonderLab
attractions.tips: Tickets are available at the door or online, but general admission may only be purchased at the door according to some site sections WonderLab. Metered street parking is available, or you can find free two-hour parking a short walk away Yelp. Be aware that Science Sprouts Place and Sprouts Lab close 15 minutes before the museum's closing time WonderLab.
attractions.nearby: The museum is located directly on the B-Line Trail, adjacent to the Captain Kathryn Janeway Statue Visit Bloomington. It is also within walking distance of the Monroe County Courthouse square and the shops and restaurants of Kirkwood Avenue Wikipedia.

attractions.name: Yellowwood State Forest
attractions.description: Established in 1940, Yellowwood State Forest encompasses over 24,000 acres of diverse forest land in Brown County, including the 133-acre Yellowwood Lake. The forest was developed from federal land leased to the state and features structures built by the Civilian Conservation Corps and Works Progress Administration during the Great Depression.
attractions.hours: Forest: Open year-round, 24/7; Office/Boat Rentals: 8:00 AM - 3:30 PM (Daily May 1 - Oct 31; Mon-Fri Nov 1 - Apr 30).
attractions.admission: Free (Entrance & Parking); Boat Rentals: Rowboats/Kayaks/Paddleboats $8/hour or $20/day; Camping: Primitive (Class C) $13/night, Horseman's $16/night; Shelter: $30; Lodge/Cabin: $225; Annual Horse Tag required for bridle trails.
highlights: Yellowwood Lake (133 acres) for fishing and boating
Tecumseh Trail (42 miles) and Ten O'Clock Line Hiking Trail (20 miles)
Rare Yellowwood trees (Cladrastis kentukea) that flower every 3-5 years
Primitive camping and horsemen's camp sites (first-come, first-served)
Self-guiding interpretive trails like Jackson Creek and Resource Management
The mystery of boulders found high in the tops of three trees
attractions.tips: Campsites are walk-in only (no reservations); register at the forest office. Avoid using Jackson Creek Road as it is unpaved and difficult; use Yellowwood Road from Highway 46 instead. A valid Indiana fishing or hunting license is required for those activities. Gold panning is permitted with a free permit obtained from the office.
attractions.nearby: Brown County State Park, T.C. Steele State Historic Site, Monroe Lake, Morgan-Monroe State Forest, Village of Nashville.
